frameworks.name: recurrent neural network dynamics
frameworks.one_line: population-level model whose connectivity is engineered to implement specified task dynamics

frameworks.name: low-dimensional manifold of neural activity
frameworks.one_line: task-relevant dynamics unfold on correlated patterns forming a low-dimensional manifold

frameworks.name: drift-diffusion process
frameworks.one_line: ring-attractor working-memory dynamics implemented as drift-diffusion on a ring manifold

frameworks.name: input-driven cognitive flexibility
frameworks.one_line: inputs reshape network dynamics to switch between task computations

core_question: How can RNN connectivity be designed so that its dynamics implement a specified task in an interpretable way?
key_assumptions: neural computations can be cast as dynamics on low-dimensional manifolds
task-relevant dynamics can be specified a priori and inverted to connectivity
manifold shape and dynamics together determine network capacity
manipulations: specification of task manifold shape (e.g., ring)
specification of dynamics on the manifold (e.g., drift-diffusion)
inputs that reconfigure dynamics for different tasks
representation geometry varied to probe capacity
key_findings: method synthesizes RNN connectivity from prescribed manifold and dynamics
working memory implemented as drift-diffusion over a ring manifold
inputs can flexibly switch the network between dynamical regimes
representation geometry trades off against network capacity
interpretations: interpretable RNNs can be engineered top-down from task-relevant manifolds and dynamics
neural computation is fruitfully framed as low-dimensional manifold dynamics
